title: Interpret Tote Fullness Thresholds And Pickup Priority From Volumetric Data
summary: Use the source-described tote fullness thresholds to interpret whether a tote is in a lower-priority pickup state, a higher-priority pickup state, or a full state that closes the chute. The source explains that software determines tote fullness from accumulated parcel dimension and weight data, with dimension scaling by package type such as boxes and bags.
when_to_use: Use when support or operations personnel need to interpret a tote's fullness status or relative pickup priority from the software-reported volumetric/fullness value using the threshold meanings described in the training source.
not_for: Do not use to set or change configured thresholds.
Do not use to invent exact configured thresholds beyond the example values discussed in the source.
Do not use as a tote dispatch, exchange, or AGV movement procedure.

roles_and_responsibilities.responsibility: Interpret the reported tote fullness value using the source-described threshold meanings and record the resulting state.

safety_notes: This is a reference procedure for interpretation of software-reported tote fullness states.
When a tote is at the full threshold, the source states the chute is closed and the destination is marked unavailable so no more packages are diverted there during exchange.
access_or_tools_needed: Access to the software view or data showing tote fullness or volumetric status
Documented threshold meanings from the training source

access_required: Access to a software view, HMI, or data source that shows tote fullness or volumetric status
system_preconditions: The tote to be interpreted can be identified in system data
A tote fullness or volumetric value is available for review

visual_references.description: Training slide describing tote fullness calculation, pretty full and full thresholds, and chute closure behavior when full.

steps.title: Identify the tote to interpret
steps.purpose: Ensure the correct tote is being evaluated before interpreting fullness state.
steps.instruction: Identify the tote whose fullness needs to be interpreted in the available software or data view.
steps.expected_result: A specific tote is selected or identified for review.
steps.healthy_condition: The tote to be evaluated is clearly identified in system data.
steps.failure_condition: The tote cannot be uniquely identified or the displayed tote does not match the tote under review.

actions.label: Identify tote
actions.exact_action: Identify the tote whose fullness needs to be interpreted.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop or escalate if the tote cannot be identified in the available software or data view.

source_evidence.text: Identify the tote whose fullness needs to be interpreted.

steps.title: Review the software-calculated fullness value
steps.purpose: Obtain the fullness or volumetric value that will be compared to the documented threshold meanings.
steps.instruction: Check the tote fullness or volumetric value calculated by software from accumulated parcel dimension data. Note that the source says package type scaling differs for boxes and bags.
steps.expected_result: A current fullness or volumetric value is available for interpretation, with awareness that package type affects scaling.
steps.healthy_condition: The software shows a fullness-related value for the tote and the interpretation accounts for package-type scaling described by the source.
steps.failure_condition: No fullness value is available, or the observed behavior suggests the value may not align with the source-described calculation basis.

actions.label: Check fullness value
actions.exact_action: Check the tote fullness or volumetric value calculated by software from accumulated parcel dimension data, noting that the source says package type scaling differs for boxes and bags.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Look for the slide content stating that software updates tote cube or fullness using dimension and weight data, with scaling based on package type such as boxes and bags.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop or escalate if no tote fullness or volumetric value is available.
Stop or escalate if observed fullness behavior does not align with the source-described threshold meanings.

source_evidence.text: Check the tote fullness or volumetric value calculated by software from accumulated parcel dimension data, noting that the source says package type scaling differs for boxes and bags.

source_ref.quote_or_summary: Software determines how full a tote is using weight and dimensions; dimensions are scaled by package type. Two thresholds exist: pretty full and full.

steps.title: Compare the value to the threshold bands
steps.purpose: Classify the tote state and relative pickup priority using the threshold meanings described in the source.
steps.instruction: Compare the observed fullness to the source-described threshold bands: over about 60% indicates pretty full and pickup should occur, over about 70% indicates higher pickup priority than lower percentages, and about 80% indicates full.
steps.expected_result: The tote is classified as pretty full, higher-priority for pickup, or full based on the source-described examples.
steps.healthy_condition: The observed fullness value maps cleanly to one of the source-described threshold meanings.
steps.failure_condition: The observed fullness value or tote behavior does not align with the source-described threshold meanings.

actions.label: Interpret threshold band
actions.exact_action: Compare the observed fullness to the source-described threshold bands: over about 60% indicates pretty full and pickup should occur, over about 70% indicates higher pickup priority than lower percentages, and about 80% indicates full.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Look for the slide and nearby transcript references to pretty full and full thresholds, and the retrieval text noting approximately 60%, 70%, and 80% fullness behavior.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop or escalate if the observed fullness behavior does not align with the source-described threshold meanings.
Stop or escalate if interpretation would require inventing exact configured thresholds beyond the example values discussed in the source.

source_evidence.text: Compare the observed fullness to the source-described threshold bands: over about 60% indicates pretty full and pickup should occur, over about 70% indicates higher pickup priority than lower percentages, and about 80% indicates full.
Nearby transcript also discusses priority behavior around approximately 60 percent, 70 percent, and 80 percent fullness, chute closure, and issues caused by missing dimension or weight data.
Tote fullness metric based on accumulated parcel dimensions and package-type scaling, with pretty full around 60%, higher priority around 70%, and full around 80%.

steps.title: Verify chute-closed state when full
steps.purpose: Confirm the source-described system behavior associated with the full threshold.
steps.instruction: If the tote is at the full threshold, verify whether the chute is already closed as described by the source.
steps.expected_result: A tote interpreted as full is also confirmed against the source-described chute-closed behavior.
steps.healthy_condition: At the full threshold, the chute is closed and the destination is unavailable so no more packages are diverted there during exchange.
steps.failure_condition: The tote appears to be at the full threshold but the chute behavior does not match the source-described closed or unavailable state.

actions.label: Verify chute closed
actions.exact_action: If the tote is at the full threshold, verify whether the chute is already closed as described by the source.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Look for the training slide or figure content showing pretty full versus full threshold behavior and the statement that when a tote is full the chute is unavailable or closed.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop or escalate if the tote is at the full threshold but the chute is not closed or unavailable as described by the source.
Stop or escalate if observed fullness behavior does not align with the source-described threshold meanings.

source_evidence.text: If the tote is at the full threshold, verify whether the chute is already closed as described by the source. [Image support needed: training slide or figure showing pretty full versus full threshold behavior and chute close state.]

source_ref.quote_or_summary: When a tote is full, we tell Chat that the chute is unavailable so that no packages come down while doing an exchange; at full threshold the chute is closed.

source_evidence.text: Full tote status causes chute closure and destination unavailability, communicated through chat so no additional packages are diverted during tote exchange.

steps.title: Record the interpreted tote state
steps.purpose: Capture the interpretation outcome using only source-supported meanings.
steps.instruction: Record the interpreted tote state and relative pickup priority using only the source-described threshold meanings.
steps.expected_result: The tote is documented as pretty full, higher-priority for pickup, or full with chute closed, based on the source.
steps.healthy_condition: The recorded state matches the observed fullness value and uses only source-supported threshold meanings.
steps.failure_condition: The recorded state includes unsupported assumptions, invented thresholds, or does not match the observed fullness value.

actions.label: Document interpretation
actions.exact_action: Record the interpreted tote state and relative pickup priority using only the source-described threshold meanings.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop or escalate if the interpretation requires unsupported assumptions or invented exact thresholds.
Stop or escalate if the observed fullness behavior does not align with the source-described threshold meanings.

source_evidence.text: Record the interpreted tote state and relative pickup priority using only the source-described threshold meanings.
Do not invent exact configured thresholds beyond the example values discussed in the source.

success_criteria: The tote can be classified using the source-described threshold meanings.
The user can distinguish a pretty full state, a higher-priority pickup state, and a full state.
If the tote is full, the interpretation includes the source-described chute-closed or unavailable condition.
healthy_conditions: A tote fullness or volumetric value is available in software.
The fullness value can be interpreted using the source-described threshold bands.
Package-type scaling is considered as part of the source-described fullness calculation.
At the full threshold, the chute is closed or unavailable as described by the source.
failure_conditions: No fullness or volumetric value is available for the tote.
Observed fullness behavior does not align with the source-described threshold meanings.
Interpretation would require inventing exact configured thresholds not supported by the source.
A tote interpreted as full does not show the source-described chute-closed or unavailable behavior.
escalation_guidance: Escalate if the observed fullness behavior does not align with the source-described threshold meanings.
Escalate if a tote appears full but the chute is not closed or unavailable as described by the source.
Escalate if interpretation depends on exact configured thresholds that are not provided in this source.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Training slide describing tote fullness calculation, threshold behavior, and chute closure when full.

source_refs.quote_or_summary: Software determines how full a tote is using weight and dimensions; dimensions are scaled by package type. Two thresholds exist: pretty full and full.
When a tote is full, we tell Chat that the chute is unavailable so that no packages come down while doing an exchange; at full threshold the chute is closed.

missing_details: The source does not identify a specific software screen name for viewing tote fullness.
The source provides conversational example thresholds around 60%, 70%, and 80%, but does not establish site-specific configured values in this packet.
The source does not define a formal recording location or documentation system for saving the interpreted tote state.
The source does not provide exact operator role boundaries beyond the candidate's likely role.
